title: Practising sounds and letters – Tau 1 - Ministry of Education
content:
Te whakaharatau i ngā oro me ngā pū i Tau 1

## Practising sounds and letters – Tau 1
Use these slides to help your mokopuna recognise letters and sounds by looking at pictures and words.

We’re inviting whānau to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## Practising sounds and letters #
When a mokopuna is learning to read, their kaiako will show them how sounds match with letters and how to combine these to read words. They will start with simple sounds and letters, then move on to more complex ones.
These slides have the sounds and letters that are taught in the first year of kura. You can use them at home to practise the sounds and letters mokopuna have been learning at kura.
Practise is important when learning sounds and letters.
Practising the sounds your mokopuna has learned, for up to 10 minutes a day, will help them to remember what they have been learning at kura.
The slides are best viewed full screen. Select the 3 dots at the bottom of the slides and then select 'Enter full screen'. The resources may not be in accessible formats.
